seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Azure AI Agent Service Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Azure AI Agent Service agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Azure AI Agent Service, AI agent hallucinations manifest as agents generating ungrounded responses from unreliable tool outputs like Bing Search or file retrievals, bypassing enterprise validation. Common issues include outdated grounding data, policy-violating tool results, and metadata pollution from Azure file search, leading to confidently wrong enterprise decisions. Vex integration as middleware enables real-time hallucination detection in the agent processing pipeline.

howItManifests.scenario: Bing Search grounding returns outdated product pricing
howItManifests.symptom: Agent confidently states 'iPhone 16 Pro: $899' when actual price is $1199
howItManifests.impact: Enterprise loses $300k in misquoted B2B deals

howItManifests.scenario: File search returns Azure metadata instead of document content
howItManifests.symptom: Agent cites 'LastModified: 2024-01-15' instead of contract terms
howItManifests.impact: Legal team signs contract missing key indemnity clauses

howItManifests.scenario: Tool execution ignores enterprise PII redaction policy
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns customer SSNs in sales summary

howItManifests.impact: Customer support gives wrong refund amounts

detection.name: Bing Search Freshness Validation
detection.description: Detects hallucination from outdated Bing grounding data by checking publication dates

detection.name: Azure File Metadata Pollution Check
detection.description: Identifies when file search returns Azure blob metadata instead of content

detection.name: Token Limit Overflow Detection
detection.description: Monitors agent thread token usage against limits with degradation detection

fixes.name: Freshness-Aware Grounding Filter
fixes.description: Filters Bing search results older than 3 days and forces re-grounding

fixes.name: Azure File Content Extractor
fixes.description: Strips metadata and extracts actual document content from file search
fixes.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentClient
from azure.storage.blob import BlobServiceClient
import re

def content_extractor_middleware(response):
    if 'azure://' in response.content:
        # Extract blob content
        blob_client = BlobServiceClient.from_connection_string('your-storage-connection')
        blob_url = re.search(r'azure://([^\s]+)', response.content).group(1)
        blob_content = blob_client.get_blob_client(blob_url).download_blob().readall()
        
        # Remove metadata pollution
        clean_content = re.sub(r'LastModified|Content-MD5|Etag', '', str(blob_content))
        response.content = f"Document content: {clean_content[:4000]}..."
    
    return response

fixes.name: Token-Aware Thread Manager
fixes.description: Gracefully degrades when approaching token limits
fixes.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentClient

def token_manager_middleware(request):
    client = AgentClient.from_connection_string('your-connection-string')
    thread_stats = client.get_thread_stats(request.thread_id)
    
    if thread_stats.total_tokens > thread_stats.max_tokens * 0.8:
        # Archive old messages and summarize
        old_messages = client.list_thread_messages(request.thread_id, limit=50)
        summary = client.create_summary(old_messages)
        
        # Clear thread history, inject summary
        client.clear_thread_history(request.thread_id)
        client.add_system_message(request.thread_id, f"Previous conversation summary: {summary}")
        
        request.metadata['token_degradation'] = True
    
    return request

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as middleware in Azure AI Agent Service pipelines, automatically detecting hallucinations across Bing grounding, file search pollution, and token overflows. It runs 17+ hallucination detectors in parallel, scoring responses with enterprise-grounded confidence metrics. 

Vex's Azure-native SDK (`pip install vex-sdk azure-ai-projects`) wraps agent calls with zero-config hallucination guards, automatically applying fixes like freshness filtering and content extraction. Remediation happens inline - bad responses get re-grounded before reaching users.

The platform provides dashboards showing hallucination rates by agent/thread, with one-click policy deployment across entire Azure AI Projects deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import VexGuard
from azure.ai.projects import AgentClient

# Install: pip install vex-sdk azure-ai-projects
vex = VexGuard(
    azure_project_id="your-project-id",
    detectors=[
        "bing_freshness",
        "file_metadata_pollution", 
        "token_overflow",
        "pii_leakage",
        "policy_violation"
    ],
    auto_fix=True
)

# Wrap existing Azure agent client
azure_client = AgentClient.from_connection_string('your-connection-string')
protected_client = vex.wrap(azure_client)

# All agent calls now auto-protected
response = protected_client.create_thread(
    instructions="Customer support agent"
)

if vex.hallucination_detected(response):
    print(f"Blocked: {vex.violation_details(response)}")
    fixed_response = vex.auto_remediate(response)
else:
    print("✅ Response verified safe")

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add `vex.wrap(your_azure_client)` to protect all your Azure AI Agents from hallucinations in 5 minutes
